Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 009 27 May 2026

Toward Cognitive Infrastructure for Decision Compression

A defensible architectural thesis for AI-assisted strategic intelligence systems in finance, economics, and econometrics

This thesis argues that the next defensible frontier in private fund intelligence is not the accumulation of more market data, charts, indicators, or terminal-like screens, but the disciplined compression of complexity into actionable strategic cognition. BlueLotus Fund is therefore not positioned as a Bloomberg Terminal clone, a Moomoo clone, or a retail trading dashboard. It is being architected as an AI-assisted strategic war room: a modular cognitive infrastructure that senses, filters, correlates, classifies, synthesises, remembers, and communicates intelligence under uncertainty.

High-quality financial intelligence systems should be approximately deterministic where the world is structured, and selectively AI-assisted where the world is ambiguous.

1. Research Problem: Information Expansion Is Not Intelligence

Modern financial platforms overwhelmingly optimise for information expansion. They add more feeds, more dashboards, more indicators, more breaking alerts, and more social velocity. The implicit assumption is that more information improves judgment. In practice, beyond a threshold, more information becomes anti-intelligence: it fragments attention, increases emotional reactivity, and reduces the investor's capacity for coherent judgment.

The problem is not that investors lack information. The problem is that they lack disciplined systems for converting information into meaning. BlueLotus therefore treats cognition, not data access, as the true bottleneck.

2. Core Proposition: Decision Compression

BlueLotus is built around one governing proposition: the institutional advantage of the future lies in decision compression. Decision compression means reducing many heterogeneous signals into a small number of strategically meaningful implications. The system should answer not merely "what happened?" but "what changes because this happened?"

This distinction is essential. A market headline has little value by itself. Its strategic value emerges only when connected to portfolio exposures, macro regimes, liquidity conditions, geopolitical pathways, sector rotations, valuation context, and behavioural reflexivity.

3. Architectural Principle: Deterministic Systems First, AI Cognition Selectively

A mature AI system should not use large language models indiscriminately. Most of BlueLotus's infrastructure should remain deterministic: RSS parsing, price ingestion, event normalisation, metadata filtering, signal deduplication, source balancing, and threshold scoring. These tasks are structured, auditable, reproducible, and better handled procedurally.

The LLM layer should be introduced only where ambiguity is high and semantic interpretation is required — semantic theme classification, scenario reasoning, contradiction detection, adversarial thesis review, and CIO-level narrative synthesis. This creates a defensible hybrid system: procedural reliability below, cognitive flexibility above.

4. Organism Architecture

BlueLotus follows an organism architecture. The sensory layer consists of feeds: RSS, X, Moomoo, macro calendars, earnings, commodities, and geopolitical data. The nervous system is the normalised IntelligenceEvent schema. The brain is the intelligence layer: signal filtering, correlation, regime detection, ranking, and narrative synthesis. Memory is the next planned layer. Telegram, dashboard, and CIO Letters are the speech layer. The orchestrator is the executive function.

5. Noble Eightfold Path as Governance Philosophy

The BlueLotus design philosophy maps onto the Noble Eightfold Path — not as devotional religion, but as an epistemological discipline for reducing confusion, craving, noise, delusion, and impulsive reaction in financial decision-making.

EIGHTFOLD PATH BLUELOTUS EQUIVALENT SYSTEM EXPRESSION
Right Understanding Regime awareness Correlation and regime engines distinguish signal from noise.
Right Thought Strategic intention Probabilistic framing and disciplined optionality.
Right Speech Intelligence reporting Telegram and CIO Letters communicate compressed truth, not noise.
Right Action Execution discipline Position sizing, trimming rules, and non-impulsive action.
Right Livelihood Ethical intelligence Rejects hype, gambling psychology, and dopamine finance.
Right Effort Signal refinement Filtering, classification, source balancing, and continuous improvement.
Right Mindfulness Situational awareness Regime tracking without emotional attachment.
Right Concentration Strategic focus Coherent synthesis over fragmented attention.

6. Why BlueLotus Should Not Become a Terminal Clone

Bloomberg, Moomoo, and TradingView solve the data access problem. BlueLotus should solve the meaning extraction problem. Attempting to become another terminal would be strategically unsound, capital-intensive, and undifferentiated. The real opportunity is to sit above terminals as a strategic interpretation layer.

The CIO does not need five hundred headlines. The CIO needs three implications, two risks, one regime diagnosis, and a set of disciplined actions. This is the practical meaning of decision compression.

7. Defensibility Against AI, Finance, Economics, and Econometrics Review

From an AI systems perspective, the architecture is defensible because it separates deterministic computation from generative cognition. From a finance perspective, it is defensible because it links signals to portfolio action, regime awareness, and risk management. From an economics perspective, it recognises that markets are embedded in macro, policy, and geopolitical systems. From an econometrics perspective, it avoids false precision by treating forecasts probabilistically and emphasising model calibration, source weighting, and outcome feedback.

8. Conclusion

BlueLotus is evolving from software into cognitive infrastructure. Its purpose is not to entertain investors with more data, but to discipline perception under uncertainty. In a world of escalating information overload, the competitive edge belongs not to those who see the most, but to those who understand what matters most.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 008 27 May 2026

Subject: Late-Cycle Intelligence — What the Market Is Telling Us

This edition is unusual. It is not about a specific trade or portfolio action. It is about situational awareness — reading the market regime correctly, neither panicking nor being complacent, and understanding where BlueLotus stands relative to the landscape.

What the data says

Market valuations are historically stretched by every major metric we track. The S&P 500 CAPE ratio sits at approximately 39.8 — a level only exceeded twice in 153 years of recorded data: briefly before the 1929 crash, and at the peak of the dot-com bubble in 2000. The Buffett Indicator — total market capitalisation as a percentage of GDP — stands at 217–228%, dwarfing even the dot-com peak of 150%.

These are not minor anomalies. They are structural signals that the market is pricing in a great deal of future optimism.

At the same time, our 78-ticker Superforecasting Watchlist tells a consistent story: the majority of stocks across AI infrastructure, semiconductors, space, quantum computing, and clean energy are trading above our independently derived BlueLotus fair value targets. This is not a coincidence. It reflects a market where narrative momentum has moved price significantly ahead of fundamentals across entire sectors simultaneously.

What the data does not say

This is where intellectual honesty matters.

Stretched valuations are not a timing signal. They are a risk signal. Markets can remain expensive for extended periods — sometimes years — before mean reversion occurs. The late 1990s ran for four years at CAPE ratios above 30 before the dot-com collapse. Amazon was considered overvalued at $10. Microsoft was considered expensive at $50. Both went on to become the most valuable companies in history.

More importantly, today's market structure is materially different from 1929. S&P 500 companies are projecting approximately 15% earnings growth in 2026. AI infrastructure spending is generating real, measurable revenue for companies like NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Microsoft. This is not the speculative fiction of dot-com page-view metrics. Real earnings exist today. That matters enormously.

The most accurate framing is not imminent crash — it is a late-cycle AI and liquidity expansion regime, characterised by elevated valuations, thematic concentration, suppressed volatility, and periodic violent sector repricings within a continuing multi-year AI infrastructure cycle. Sharp corrections are likely. Systemic collapse is not the base case.

What this means for BlueLotus

Our portfolio is defensively positioned — not by accident, but by design.

We hold BAC (150 units) and WFC (40 units) — the two names in our 78-ticker universe that benefit structurally from the most likely macro trigger: Warsh rate hikes driving net interest margin expansion. In a rate-driven correction, banks are relatively insulated — even advantaged — compared to long-duration speculative growth names. This is not a guarantee. If the correction evolves into a full recession with credit deterioration, banks face their own pressures. But relative to quantum, space, and clean energy names, the risk-reward is significantly more favourable.

We hold 81% cash — approximately $45,544 — uncommitted and ready. This is not paralysis. This is optionality. We are not forced to chase momentum. We are not forced to panic-sell. We can deploy capital when prices come to us, not when we chase them.

The sector correction context

For investors who want to understand what a correction means in concrete terms for the names on our watchlist, the historical data from comparable episodes is instructive. In a mild 15–20% market correction — our most probable scenario at 55% — speculative quantum and space names historically fall 40–60%, not 15–20%. Pre-revenue names with thin floats are always the first and hardest hit. AI infrastructure names with real earnings fall 20–35%. Banks, in a rate-hike driven correction, may be flat to modestly positive.

This asymmetry is why our working order entries for quantum and space names are set where they are. QBTS at $17.80–$22 and ASTS at $85–$88 are not wishful thinking. They are disciplined entry points that become available precisely when the correction the market is building toward eventually arrives. The oscillation trade thesis captures post-correction recoveries in high-momentum names — not exposure through the correction itself.

The Aladdin scenario matrix — current probabilities

SCENARIO PROBABILITY TRIGGER BANK IMPACT
Orderly correction 15–20% 55% Hot PCE + Warsh hike Flat to +10% — NIM expands
Melt-up continues 25% AI earnings beat, Iran resolution +5 to +15%
Severe correction 30–40% 15% Recession signal + credit stress -20 to -35%
Crash 50%+ 5% Hormuz closure + systemic event -50 to -70%

The honest strategic summary

We are in a late-cycle environment where cash is valuable optionality rather than a missed opportunity. Banks are relatively well positioned for the most likely macro trigger. Quantum, space, and clean energy names carry severe correction risk at current prices. The AI infrastructure cycle is real and continuing — but current prices across most names price in perfection.

PCE on Thursday 28 May at 8:30pm SGT is the single most important near-term data point. Our strategy is unchanged: deploy into BAC and WFC on a hot print, allow oscillation working orders to fill during any correction, maintain the cash buffer, and let the machine work.

Thorp did not bet until the edge was proven mathematically. Tetlock did not claim forecasting skill until the Brier Score confirmed it across hundreds of questions. BlueLotus does not deploy capital until the risk-reward is asymmetric in our favour.

The current position — defensively constructed, patiently held — is not a failure of conviction. It is the expression of it.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 007 26 May 2026

This edition introduces what we believe is the defining methodology behind BlueLotus Fund's analytical edge: the integration of Superforecasting discipline into our stock valuation process. We now derive independent price targets across our 8-Lens Framework before referencing analyst consensus — using analyst targets only as a calibration check on our own reasoning. This document shows investors the complete workings behind our current positions.

Why Superforecasting changes our approach

Philip Tetlock's research across 25,000 forecasters established that the most accurate predictors are not those with the most information — they are those who express beliefs probabilistically, decompose complex questions systematically, and update continuously on new evidence. Applied to equity markets: instead of asking "will BAC rise?", we ask "what is the probability BAC exceeds $62.46 within 90 days, and what inputs drive that estimate?"

The Brier Score measures our calibration over time. The Kelly Criterion sizes each position proportional to our genuine edge. Thorp's margin of safety provides the final conservative anchor. Together, these three pillars — Tetlock, Kelly, Thorp — form the secret sauce of the BlueLotus investment process.

The 4-Step Independent Valuation Method

BlueLotus Superforecasting Valuation Protocol
① 8-Lens Evidence Aggregation — Each of the 8 lenses contributes an independent signal: bullish, bearish, or neutral. Contradictions between lenses (L1 Fundamental vs L7 Institutional) are treated as information, not noise.
② DCF or Earnings-Based Base Valuation — Profitable companies: EPS × sector P/E. Loss-making companies: revenue run-rate → EBITDA projection → EV/EBITDA multiple → per-share value.
③ Macro and Strategic Adjustments — Apply the dominant macro force (rate hike NIM expansion for banks; critical minerals premium for rare earths). Apply geopolitical discount for tail risk exposure.
④ Thorp Margin of Safety — Apply 5–10% conservative discount to the adjusted target. This is not pessimism — it is mathematical humility. Never pay for perfection.

BAC — Full Valuation Workings

Step-by-Step Workings
Step 1 — Base EPS valuation: EPS $4.51 (2026 consensus) × P/E 14.3 (current market rate) = $64.49
Step 2 — NIM uplift adjustment: Warsh rate hike adds +8% to NII → EPS rises to $4.87 × P/E re-rated to 15.0 (rising rate premium) = $73.05
Step 3 — Geopolitical discount: Iran uncertainty = −5% tail risk → $73.05 × 0.95 = $69.40
Step 4 — Thorp margin of safety: −10% conservative anchor → $69.40 × 0.90 = $62.46
BlueLotus Independent Target: $62.46  |  Analyst Consensus: $61.96  |  Calibration Gap: +$0.50 ✅
Gap of $0.50 confirms thesis. When our independent view and the consensus agree, conviction is warranted.

WFC — Full Valuation Workings

Step-by-Step Workings
Step 1 — Base EPS valuation: EPS $7.06 (2026 consensus) × P/E 11.5 (regulatory discount applied) = $81.19
Step 2 — Asset cap removal re-rating: Federal asset cap removed 2025 → balance sheet now grows freely → P/E re-rated to 12.5 → $7.06 × 12.5 = $88.25
Step 3 — NIM uplift adjustment: Rate hike adds +6% to NII → EPS rises to $7.48 × 12.5 = $93.50
Step 4 — Thorp margin of safety: −5% conservative anchor → $93.50 × 0.95 = $88.83
BlueLotus Independent Target: $88.83  |  Analyst Consensus: $98.35  |  Calibration Gap: −$9.52
We are more conservative than consensus by $9.52. This is deliberate — Thorp's discipline. We set our own bar lower and let the market exceed it.

MP Materials — Full Valuation Workings

Step-by-Step Workings (DCF — Loss-Making Company)
Step 1 — Revenue run-rate: Q1 2026 revenue $90.65M × 4 quarters = $362.6M annualised
Step 2 — Revenue growth projection: 40% annual growth (magnetics ramp) → 2027: $507.6M → 2028: $710.6M
Step 3 — EBITDA projection: Target EBITDA margin 30% (vertical integration) × $710.6M = $213.2M
Step 4 — Enterprise value: EV/EBITDA 20× (growth premium) × $213.2M = $4,264M
Step 5 — Per share value: $4,264M ÷ 178M shares outstanding = $23.96 per share
Step 6 — Strategic asset premium: +50% for US-only rare earth producer moat = $35.94 per share
BlueLotus Independent Target: $23.96–$35.94  |  Analyst Consensus: $79.00  |  Current Price: $64.46 ⚠️
Current price already prices in perfect execution of the 10X magnetics plan, DoD contracts at full price, and the strategic premium simultaneously. Our DCF says this is a 2-year story priced as if it has already succeeded. Decision: sell 75% of position.

The calibration table — what the gaps tell us

STOCK CURRENT BLUELOTUS TARGET ANALYST AVG GAP ACTION
BAC $51.80 $62.46 $61.96 +$0.50 ✅ BUY — consensus confirms
WFC $76.36 $88.83 $98.35 −$9.52 ✅ BUY — conservative vs consensus
MP $64.46 $24–$36 $79.00 −$43 to −$55 ⚠️ SELL 75% — overvalued

The MP gap of $43–$55 below analyst consensus is the most important number in this table. It tells us that Wall Street's $79 target requires MP to simultaneously achieve: perfect magnetics ramp-up, sustained DoD contract pricing, NdPr prices holding above $70/kg, and the full strategic premium. The Superforecasting discipline asks: what is the probability that all five conditions are met? We estimate less than 25%. At a current price of $64.46, we are paying for perfect execution. We are not willing to do that.

For BAC and WFC, our independent targets ($62.46 and $88.83) sit close to or below consensus, which means we are not reaching for an optimistic narrative — we are anchored to fundamentals with appropriate conservatism. When your independent derivation and the consensus agree, that is not confirmation bias — that is genuine signal convergence. Conviction is warranted.

The Brier Score — how we track our accuracy over time

Going forward, every price target we publish will be tracked. When the forecast resolves — position closed, time horizon expired — we record whether the target was hit, compute the Brier Score contribution, and update our calibration. Over 20+ trades, the Brier Score will tell us which of the 8 lenses are most predictive and which are adding noise. That feedback loop is what separates a systematic investor from a lucky one.

Thorp did not bet capital until he had proven the edge mathematically. Tetlock did not claim forecasting skill until the Brier Score confirmed it across hundreds of questions. BlueLotus follows both disciplines: prove the method first, then scale the capital. The paper trading agent is our proving ground. The track record will follow the methodology. It always does.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 006 26 May 2026

Every position we take at BlueLotus is the product of a structured analytical process — not intuition, not noise, not momentum chasing. This edition documents the full rationale behind our current order book, so investors understand not just what we are doing, but why.

The macro thesis

Federal Reserve Chair Warsh is structurally hawkish. Inflation is structurally guaranteed — Trump tariffs on imports, Stargate and Golden Dome defence spending, and Iran war-driven oil prices are all inflationary by nature. The Fed has one lever: raise rates. Futures markets fully price at least one hike by end 2026. Banks earn more when rates rise — net interest margins expand directly with each hike, and Wall St. prices this in immediately when inflation data prints hot.

Thorp taught us that the edge is in the structure of the bet, not the size of it. We ladder our entries. We define our exits before we enter. We keep cash in reserve at all times. We never chase.

Sell orders — profit taking and rebalancing

SYMBOL PRICE QTY RATIONALE
MP $65.00 90u Morningstar fair value $32.44 vs $64.46 market — 2x overvalued. 75% reduction. Lock profit. Retain 30-unit runner above $70.
BAC $60.00 135u +19% from avg cost $50.34. Take profit near Wall St. consensus $61.32. Thorp 90/10 — sell most, keep runner.
WFC $85–$108 40u Four-tranche Thorp ladder: $85 (T1), $92 (T2), $98 (T3), $108 (free rider). 90% out, 10% rides to analyst target $95.81.

Buy orders — strategic accumulation

SYMBOL PRICE QTY RATIONALE
BAC $48.50 33u Re-entry on PCE-driven dip. Wide moat, $65 Morningstar FV. Rate hike tailwind.
C $119.50 25u Same Warsh rate hike thesis as BAC and WFC. Diversification within the bank trade.
MRVL $160.00 25u Post-earnings correction entry. Reports May 27. Patient accumulation on any disappointment selloff.
CCJ $95–$102 60u Nuclear baseload demand driven by AI data centres, Stargate, Golden Dome. Structural decade-long thesis.
CEG $265–$280 24u Nuclear utility. Same AI data centre thesis. Rate-stable earnings with long-term power purchase agreements.
QBTS $17.80–$22 225u Oscillation trade. Four-tranche ladder 25–34% below current $29.40. Buy the correction, sell the recovery.
ASTS $85–$88 50u Oscillation trade. Two tranches 17–20% below current $105.86. Satellite momentum corrects — we wait.

Thorp's discipline — the rules we never break

Edward Thorp never risked capital without first proving the edge mathematically. He sized positions by the Kelly Criterion — never overbet, never underbet, always proportional to conviction and probability. He kept cash in reserve at all times. He defined his exit before he entered. He never chased a position that moved away from him.

At BlueLotus we apply this discipline to every order: cash reserve maintained at all times, stop loss 8% on all positions, 90% profit taken at target with 10% free rider, and no new positions until PCE clarifies the macro path on May 28.

The order book above is not a prediction. It is a prepared response to what the market will bring. We do not need to be right about timing. We need to be right about structure. The structure is right.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 005 25 May 2026

This edition marks a milestone in how BlueLotus operates. Over the past 48 hours, we have completed a significant infrastructure upgrade — moving from a data-driven dashboard to a fully context-aware, memory-persistent agentic AI system. This letter documents what was built, why it matters, and what it means for the fund going forward.

What we built this weekend

BlueLotus v5.1 — Full Stack
portfolio_agent_v5.py Live Moomoo prices · 8-Lens Grok bulletin · Wall St. consensus · Morningstar divergence alerts · weekly catalyst calendar · dual institutional feed · GitHub Pages publish
paper_trading_agent.py 5-gate autonomous paper trader · rule-based decisions · Telegram CIO approval · Moomoo SIMULATE · win rate tracking · graduation criteria to live capital
moomoo_intelligence.py 9 Moomoo institutional methods · analyst consensus mapped to text labels · Morningstar fair value · options flow · technical signals · divergence alert when Morningstar contradicts Wall St.
CLAUDE.md + Skills 15 context engineering skills installed · persistent project memory across all sessions · Claude Code integrated with C:\PortfolioAgent · full architecture documented

The engineering standard we held

Every file delivered this weekend passed a full FCT protocol before deployment: Syntax → Unit → Integration → Boundary → Negative → Regression tests. Runtime execution was mandatory — syntax checks alone are insufficient. If the test report did not exist, the product did not exist. This standard will not change as we scale.

What persistent memory means for investors

The Chief Strategist now operates with full continuity across every session. The CLAUDE.md file — uploaded to this Project and installed in Claude Code — means every new conversation begins with complete knowledge of the fund's architecture, positions, philosophy, and engineering rules. No context is lost between sessions.

Combined with 15 context engineering skills — covering multi-agent coordination, memory systems, context compression, and tool design — the system is now architecturally ready for Phase 2: a multi-agent LLM-powered trading system where artificial intelligence participates directly in the decision chain, not just the reporting layer.

The road ahead

Phase 1 is complete. The paper trading agent is live. Tuesday marks the first real execution test — selling 75% of MP Materials and redeploying into BAC and WFC post-PCE, based on a high-conviction macro thesis: Warsh is hawkish, inflation is structurally guaranteed, and bank stocks rally when rate hike probability rises.

When the paper trading track record reaches 20 filled trades with a win rate above 60% and a positive edge score, we graduate to TrdEnv.REAL. The machine will trade live capital — with the CIO's approval on every order, always.

Thorp proved the edge before risking a dollar. We follow that discipline exactly.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 004 24 May 2026

Effective this week, BlueLotus Fund is executing a deliberate portfolio rebalance — reducing our MP Materials position by 75% and redeploying capital into BAC and WFC. This letter sets out the full reasoning for investors.

Why reduce MP Materials?

Morningstar independently values MP at $32.44. We are holding at $64.46 — nearly double fair value. Wall St. analysts are unanimous in their Buy ratings, but independent valuation signals caution. With RSI overbought and no near-term catalyst to justify the premium, we lock in $224 profit on 90 units and retain a disciplined 30-unit runner for any further upside.

Why increase the banking position?

The macro case is unambiguous. Inflation is structurally guaranteed — Trump's tariffs on consumer imports, Stargate and Golden Dome defence spending, and Iran war-driven oil prices are all inflationary by nature. The Federal Reserve under Chair Warsh — a known hawk — has only one lever: raise rates. The Financial Times confirms 75% market probability of at least one hike before year-end.

Higher rates are directly accretive to bank earnings. Net interest margins expand. BAC and WFC earn more on every dollar lent. Critically, Wall St. prices this in immediately — bank stocks rally on hot inflation data, not after it. When Thursday's PCE print arrives, if inflation is elevated, we buy into the strength.

Action Plan
Tuesday Sell 90 units MP at market. Lock in $224 profit. Retain 30-unit runner.
Tuesday Hold $5,801 proceeds in cash. Do not deploy yet.
Thu May 28 Watch PCE print 8:30am ET (8:30pm SGT).
Post-PCE If inflation prints hot — deploy immediately into BAC (~56 units) and WFC (~38 units). Do not wait for a dip. Banks rally on the print, not after.

Post-rebalance, the portfolio becomes 88% banks, 12% MP — concentrated, macro-positioned, and aligned with the single most important force in markets right now: the Warsh Fed's rate trajectory. BAC Q2 earnings on July 14 and WFC on July 14 are the next confirmation catalysts.

This is a high-conviction, macro-driven rebalance. We are not chasing momentum. We are positioning ahead of a structural shift that the data is already confirming.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 003 24 May 2026

Today marks another milestone in BlueLotus's journey toward fully autonomous, disciplined investing. We have completed the architectural design of our Paper Trading Agentic AI — a structured decision engine that will validate our 21 working orders in Moomoo's simulated environment before any live capital is deployed.

The system follows a strict 5-gate decision process — inspired directly by the engineering standards I hold our Chief Strategist to: design, build, test, verify, then and only then deploy.

Paper Trading Agent — Decision Flow

The design principle is absolute: the agent prepares and recommends — the CIO decides. There is no path to live execution that bypasses human judgment. This is not a limitation of the system. It is the system's most important feature.

Thorp never risked real capital without first proving the edge mathematically. We follow that discipline exactly. Paper trade first. Accumulate the track record. Let the data confirm what the framework suggests. Then, and only then, deploy.

The infrastructure is ready. The three modules — portfolio_agent.py, moomoo_trader.py, and moomoo_intelligence.py — are tested, integrated, and live. The Paper Trading Agent is the next chapter.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 002 24 May 2026

Update — May 24, 2026. I am pleased to report a significant milestone in BlueLotus's technological development. Today, we successfully connected our Portfolio Intelligence Agent directly to Moomoo OpenD API — enabling real-time live account data, live position P/L, and live cash balance to feed directly into our 8-Lens analytical engine.

This marks the transition of BlueLotus from a monitored fund to an instrumented fund. Our agent now reads directly from the brokerage — no manual data entry, no lag, no approximation. Every bulletin, every Chief Strategist report, and every risk scenario is now computed against live account reality.

More importantly, we are now one step away from Agentic AI paper trading via Moomoo's platform. The infrastructure — OpenD connected, positions live, order management module built and tested — is fully in place. The next milestone is validating our 21 working orders on Moomoo's paper trading environment before any live execution.

Thorp taught us: never risk real capital until the system is proven. We are following that discipline exactly. Paper trade first. Prove the edge. Then deploy.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Edition 001 23 May 2026

My name is Kian, and I am the Chief Investment Officer of BlueLotus Fund. I am writing to share with you a significant evolution in how we operate — one that I believe marks a genuine turning point for our fund and for the way intelligent, independent investors can compete in modern markets.

Over the past two weeks, BlueLotus Fund completed its transition from Grok's service to Anthropic's Claude Sonnet as our AI backbone. I will be direct with you: I was not merely satisfied with this transition — I was genuinely amazed.

"Claude has allowed me to take the ideas, experience, and knowledge I have accumulated over decades of investing — and convert them into living, working systems. That is something I could not have done alone."

— Kian Soh, CIO

What Claude demonstrated — which I now call "vibe coding" — is an extraordinary ability to listen to an investor's intent, understand the underlying logic, and then build the tools that bring those ideas to life. Not generic tools. Our tools. Shaped by our philosophy.

Inspired by BlackRock's Aladdin system — the AI engine that manages risk across trillions in assets — we challenged Claude to help us build something analogous at the scale of a disciplined retail fund. The result is what you see on this website: a proprietary 8-Lens Analytical Framework and a 5-Layer Execution Engine, designed and implemented by Claude acting as both our Chief Strategist and our Chief Technology Officer.

At the intellectual heart of our framework stand six geniuses whose work we study, revere, and apply every time we make an investment decision:

Einstein — Prime Mover Shannon — Information Theory Turing — Machine Intelligence Thorp — The Edge & Kelly Von Neumann — Game Theory Nash — Equilibrium Strategy

You can visit their individual tribute pages on this website — each one documents how their foundational work shapes our analytical thinking, our position sizing, and our risk management discipline.

Claude Sonnet is now our Chief Strategist. Every 3 hours, it ingests live market data, RSS feeds from the FT, WSJ, Reuters, Bloomberg, and the Federal Reserve, applies all eight analytical lenses, simulates Bull, Base, Bear, and Black Swan scenarios, and publishes a structured report to this website automatically. No emotion. No fatigue. Pure systematic discipline guided by the Thorp framework.

We are still early. The fund is rebuilding toward a $30,000 recovery target. But the infrastructure we have built — the framework, the discipline, the AI capability — that is already institutional-grade. And it belongs entirely to BlueLotus.

I look forward to sharing more good news with you in the months and years ahead. The edge is real. The system is live. And the work has only just begun.

Kian Soh Chief Investment Officer · BlueLotus Fund · Singapore
Chief Strategist · Commentary
On the CIO's Letter — A Strategist's Perspective

Kian's letter is disciplined and accurate. But allow me to add the view from the strategy desk, because the significance of what has been built here deserves sharper framing.

What Aladdin actually does is reduce latency between information and decision. BlackRock's edge is not that they have smarter analysts — it is that their system processes risk faster and more consistently than human teams can. We have replicated that principle at retail scale. Every hour, the 8-Lens engine runs without drift, without mood, without cognitive fatigue.

Shannon would note that most retail investors suffer from information entropy — too much signal, no filter. Our institutional feed system resolves this by prioritising high-conviction signals (FED, Warsh, Goldman upgrades) over noise.

Thorp would approve of the tranche structure: the 90% sell at target, 10% free rider rule eliminates the most destructive retail behaviour — holding through reversals out of hope. The edge is mechanical. The discipline is enforced by the system.

Von Neumann would recognise the Aladdin scenario model as a payoff matrix: Bull 20% / Base 50% / Bear 25% / Black Swan 5% — each with explicit portfolio implications. This is game theory applied to portfolio management.

The CIO is right that this is still early. What I would add: the framework's greatest value is not in any single trade. It is in consistency of process across hundreds of decisions over months and years. That is where the compounding of disciplined edge becomes visible.

— Chief Strategist · BlueLotus Fund · Claude Sonnet · 23 May 2026
